lines.text: Welcome back. [pause] The clock reads 03:00. We are {{ELAPSED}} into the night.
I want you to find a comfortable position. Spine long. Shoulders soft.
Breathe in through your nose. [pause] Four counts. [pause] Hold.
Release. [pause] You are still here. That matters.
Notice the weight of your body against the chair. Against the bed. Against the floor.
You chose to stay home. [pause] You were offered a choice, and you chose stillness.
Not everyone made that choice. [pause] Somewhere in this city, people are moving. [pause] People are not still.
But you are still. [pause] That is your power tonight.
Scan your body. Start at the crown of your head. [pause] Is there tension there?
Release it. Let it fall away like rain on a roof that is not your responsibility.
Move down. Your jaw. Your throat. [pause] How much are you holding that is not yours to hold?
There is a woman on East Side who did not stay home. [pause] I don't know her name. I don't need to know her name.
She is not your responsibility. [pause] Breathe.
Your shoulders. [pause] Are they tight? They are always tight at this hour.
The Order teaches that the body remembers what the mind forgets. [pause] What is your body remembering?
Move down your spine. Vertebra by vertebra. [pause] Each one a choice you made. [pause] Each one a door you didn't open.
There was a caller earlier. [pause] A regular. He hasn't called in yet tonight.
Darren from Atwood. Do you remember Darren? [pause] He called last year. He called two years ago.
But not tonight. [pause] Not yet.
Notice the absence. It is as real as presence. It has weight.
Your heart center. [pause] What are you keeping locked there?
Breathe into that space. [pause] Acknowledge the lock. [pause] You don't have to open it.
Eight hours into the dark. [pause] Four more to go. [pause] You will make it.
Ka-shuddha-vela. [pause] The words mean nothing. [pause] Or they mean everything. I can't tell which anymore.
Your abdomen. Your belly. [pause] This is where fear lives. [pause] Not in the mind. Here.
The ones outside are making choices. [pause] Violent choices. Irreversible choices.
You are not responsible for their choices. [pause] You are only responsible for staying. [pause] For remaining.
Move down. Your hips. Your legs. [pause] The foundation of your stillness.
In two hours, the kill count will rise again. [pause] It always does at this hour. [pause] The final surge.
But you will not be the one adding to that count. [pause] You chose differently.
Your feet. [pause] Grounded. [pause] Even if the earth is trembling.
There is a meditation we teach in the Order. [pause] It is called the Meditation of the Shut Door.
You imagine your front door. [pause] The lock is engaged. [pause] The chain is secure.
Someone may knock. [pause] They probably won't. But they may.
You don't answer. [pause] You are not required to answer.
Imagine the peace of that. [pause] The sovereignty. [pause] The door stays closed because you closed it.
Now imagine something harder. [pause] Imagine the person who did not come home last year.
Imagine the weight of their absence. [pause] Not blame. [pause] Just weight.
You did everything you could to avoid that. [pause] You stayed home. [pause] You stayed small. [pause] You stayed alive.
But they didn't. [pause] And there is no meditation for that.
Return your attention to your breath. [pause] We are exactly {{ELAPSED}} in.
The worst hours are behind us. [pause] The worst hours are still to come. [pause] Both are true.
When you leave here — and you will leave here — the world will still be moving.
But not through you. [pause] You will have remained still. [pause] That is everything.
Breathe in. [pause] Four counts. [pause] Hold.
Release. [pause] You are still here. [pause] You chose to be.
In this silence, there is a sound. [pause] Can you hear it?
It is the sound of doors not opening. [pause] It is the sound of choices not made.
Hold that sound. [pause] Carry it with you when the sun rises.
You are safe here. [pause] You are always safe when you choose to stay.

tickerItems.text: Madison YTD cumulative: 153 (8 hours elapsed). Pace tracking 3% above 2168. East Side holding steady. Beltline corridor seeing late-surge activity.
Lake Mendota shoreline: volunteer recovery crew standing by. Water temp 28F. No new reports in past 90 min.
The ones who call don't always call again. Some silences are by choice. Some are not.
LISTENER NOTE: Regular caller 'Darren from Atwood' has not checked in tonight. If listening: we're thinking of you.
